_doc: Per-slot prompt constraints enforced at runtime by utils/prompt-guard.js. Source of truth for what each slot should/shouldn't contain. Primary model: nano-banana-pro (Google). fallback_flux_kontext = option 2 retry model when nano-banana output fails QC. Update when xp/prompt-anti-patterns.md gains new rules.

slot1.reasoning: Pure white hero. Reference image carries 100% of product identity. Text only needs: pure white bg, 3/4 angle, fidelity guard.

slot2.reasoning: Wide shot. Product ~10% of frame. AI cannot resolve fine structure. Over-describing structure → model reimagines. Use Flux Kontext if possible. Put overlay bans in negative_prompt.

safeguard_phrases: product must render sharply with accurate proportions
every dimension arrow, label, and callout card text fully readable with no overlap
slot3.reasoning: LOCKED STRUCTURE (RULE-016): Combined features + size infographic. Layout = header banner top + product large on left with dimension arrows (width/height) + thickness callout bubble + N numbered callout cards (rounded squares) on right. Product structure allowed because infographic role. Budget widened from 150→180 because gộp 2 slot cũ (features + size).

slot4.reasoning: Demonstrates functional mechanism (card insertion). see_through_cutout_rule is essential here because the mechanism is the value. But still close-up, so no lifestyle lighting narrative.

safeguard_phrases: EXACTLY N equal rounded-square cells arranged in a clean grid (default 2×2, adaptive 2-4 cells based on product niche), one product instance per cell
NO extra product anywhere outside the cells — absolutely NO floating hero product in the center of the grid or between cells
NO elements overlapping between cells — each cell self-contained with its own background context
every numbered cell label and caption fully readable, no product obstructing any label
slot5.reasoning: LOCKED STRUCTURE (RULE-017): N-cell display-locations grid (default 2×2, adaptive 2-4 cells). Each cell shows the product in a different real-use display/use scenario. Numbered labels top-left + location name bottom-left. Budget widened from 70→120 because grid + labels + safeguard phrases need space. Safeguard phrases prevent AI default tendency to add a center hero product overlapping labels (bug verified on TNHG231025i621 first attempt — 5th ornament appeared in center).

slot6.reasoning: Top-down flat lay. Cavity depth CAN be shown from top-down angle. Scene has warm lighting from above. But no hand — it's a flat lay.

slot7.reasoning: Wide group emotional scene. Product small in frame, same reasoning as slot 2.

slot8.reasoning: Close-up lifestyle hero. Can show cavity + ambient lighting integration. Benchmark slot for the system.

video_scene.reasoning: Video start frame — same fidelity rules as listing slots, but slightly larger word budget because storyboard scenes need a tiny bit more environment context. Still bounded so RULE-015 holds.
